mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates strong aggregate demand, which drives corporate earnings higher and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter as the economy expands.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year correlation between GDP growth and next-quarter SPX returns is negative or insignificant (p > 0.10) over a 5-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5%, it signals labor market weakening that reduces consumer income and spending, leading to lower corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In triggered months, 20-day SPX returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10) or the Rank IC of UNRATE level in triggered periods is not negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with near-zero federal funds rate indicates easy monetary conditions fueling demand that may overheat the economy, prompting future rate hikes and depressing equity valuations over a quarter.
failure_condition: Combined signal produces positive 60d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances or the decile spread of the interaction variable fails to be negative over a 3-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation exceeds 4% year-over-year, strong nonfarm payroll growth signals an overheating labor market that forces the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, depressing equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When triggered, the average 20-day SPX return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the Rank IC of PAYEMS changes during high-inflation periods is not negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in industrial production signals a sudden economic slowdown or supply disruption that immediately reduces corporate output and earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: In triggered weeks, 5-day SPX returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10) or the proportion of negative returns is less than 55% over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts alongside rising federal funds rate indicates a housing market contraction driven by tighter monetary policy, which erodes consumer wealth and construction employment, producing negative equity returns over a month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average declining and FEDFUNDS > 0.5%
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20d returns in more than 50% of cases or the interaction decile spread is not negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising Consumer Price Index erodes real purchasing power and forces the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC of CPI level or change on 60d forward returns is positive or not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly nonfarm payroll gain below 100,000 indicates a weak labor market that fails to sustain consumer income growth, leading to lower spending and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In months with payrolls below 100k, 20-day SPX returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10) or the proportion of negative returns is below 55% over any 5-year window.
